title: Lines On Mrs. Kemble.
content:
Kemble, thou cur'st my unbelief
            Of Moses and his rod;
        At Yarico's sweet notes of grief
            The rock with tears had flow'd.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "Lines On Mrs. Kemble.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
